tasks.title: SPM Project Setup
tasks.description: Create Package.swift, directory structure, Info.plist, and a minimal compilable app entry point.
steps: Create Package.swift with macOS 14 platform, Pasty executable target, and PastyTests test target
Create directory structure: Sources/Pasty/, Sources/Pasty/Models/, Sources/Pasty/Managers/, Sources/Pasty/Views/, Tests/PastyTests/, Resources/
Create Resources/Info.plist with LSUIElement=true and bundle identifier com.pasty.app
Create Sources/Pasty/PastyApp.swift with @main App struct that creates an empty WindowGroup (placeholder)
Verify: swift build compiles successfully

tasks.title: ClipboardItem Model
tasks.description: Define the ClipboardItem struct that represents a single clipboard history entry.
steps: Create Sources/Pasty/Models/ClipboardItem.swift
Define ClipboardItem as a struct conforming to Identifiable, Equatable, Hashable, Sendable
Properties: id (UUID), content (String), timestamp (Date), contentType (enum: plainText, richText, image — start with plainText only)
Add a computed property 'preview' that returns first 80 characters of content with ellipsis if truncated
Create Tests/PastyTests/ClipboardItemTests.swift with tests for creation, preview truncation, and equality
Verify: swift test passes

tasks.title: Settings Manager
tasks.description: Create SettingsManager that stores app preferences in UserDefaults.
steps: Create Sources/Pasty/Models/SettingsManager.swift
Use @Observable macro and @MainActor
Properties: maxHistorySize (Int, default 10, range 5–50), hotkeyEnabled (Bool, default true)
Back each property with UserDefaults using computed getters/setters or @AppStorage
Add static let shared singleton
Create Tests/PastyTests/SettingsManagerTests.swift testing default values and persistence
Verify: swift test passes

tasks.title: Clipboard Manager
tasks.description: Implement ClipboardManager that polls the system clipboard and maintains an in-memory history.
steps: Create Sources/Pasty/Managers/ClipboardManager.swift
Use @Observable macro and @MainActor
Properties: history ([ClipboardItem]), lastChangeCount (Int, tracks NSPasteboard.changeCount)
Method startMonitoring(): start a Timer every 0.5s that checks NSPasteboard.general
Method stopMonitoring(): invalidate the timer
On clipboard change: create ClipboardItem, prepend to history, trim to maxHistorySize
Skip duplicates (don't add if content matches the most recent item)
Method clearHistory(): empties the history array
Create Tests/PastyTests/ClipboardManagerTests.swift testing add, dedup, max size, and clear
Verify: swift test passes

tasks.title: Status Bar Controller
tasks.description: Create the menu bar icon and dropdown menu with Show History and Settings options.
steps: Create Sources/Pasty/Managers/StatusBarController.swift
Use @MainActor class
Create NSStatusItem with systemSymbolName 'doc.on.clipboard' or 'list.clipboard'
Build NSMenu with items: 'Show Clipboard History' (Cmd+Shift+V), separator, 'Settings...', 'Quit Pasty'
Expose callbacks: onShowHistory, onShowSettings, onQuit (closures set by AppDelegate)
Verify: swift build compiles

tasks.title: Popup View (SwiftUI)
tasks.description: Build the SwiftUI view that displays the clipboard history list.
steps: Create Sources/Pasty/Views/ClipboardItemRow.swift — SwiftUI view for a single clipboard item
Show: item preview text, timestamp (relative: '2m ago'), item index number (1-based)
Add hover highlight effect
Create Sources/Pasty/Views/PopupView.swift — SwiftUI view with a List of ClipboardItemRow
Accept items: [ClipboardItem] and onSelect: (ClipboardItem) -> Void callback
Add a search/filter TextField at the top that filters items by content
Show 'No items' placeholder when history is empty
Verify: swift build compiles

tasks.title: Popup Window Controller
tasks.description: Create the NSPanel-based window that hosts the PopupView and can show/hide near the cursor.
steps: Create Sources/Pasty/Views/PopupWindowController.swift
Use @MainActor class
Create an NSPanel with styleMask [.nonactivatingPanel, .titled, .resizable, .fullSizeContentView]
Set panel.level = .floating, panel.isFloatingPanel = true
Method showPopup(items:onSelect:): host PopupView in NSHostingView, position panel near mouse cursor, order front
Method hidePopup(): orderOut the panel
Method toggle(items:onSelect:): show if hidden, hide if visible
Handle click-outside to dismiss (panel.hidesOnDeactivate or NSEvent monitor)
Verify: swift build compiles

tasks.title: Hotkey Manager (Carbon API)
tasks.description: Register and handle a global Cmd+Shift+V hotkey using the Carbon API.
steps: Create Sources/Pasty/Managers/HotkeyManager.swift
Import Carbon.HIToolbox
Use @MainActor class with a static shared instance
Method register(): call InstallEventHandler + RegisterEventHotKey for Cmd+Shift+V
Method unregister(): call UnregisterEventHotKey
Use signature 'PSTY' (0x50535459) and id 1
Store a callback closure: onHotkeyPressed: (() -> Void)?
Handle the Carbon event callback (C function pointer) — use a global/static reference to call back into Swift
Respect SettingsManager.hotkeyEnabled — only register when enabled
Verify: swift build compiles

tasks.title: Settings View (SwiftUI)
tasks.description: Build the SwiftUI settings window with history size and hotkey toggle.
steps: Create Sources/Pasty/Views/SettingsView.swift
Bind to SettingsManager.shared
Section 'General': Stepper or Slider for maxHistorySize (5–50), Toggle for hotkeyEnabled
Section 'About': App name, version, brief description
Use Form layout with GroupBox or Section styling
Verify: swift build compiles

tasks.title: Paste Action
tasks.description: Implement the paste action: write selected item to clipboard and simulate Cmd+V.
steps: Add a method to ClipboardManager: pasteItem(_ item: ClipboardItem)
Implementation: write item.content to NSPasteboard.general, update lastChangeCount to avoid re-capturing it
After writing to pasteboard, simulate Cmd+V using CGEvent (keyDown + keyUp for V key with .maskCommand)
Add a small delay (DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter 0.05s) between writing pasteboard and simulating the key
Hide the popup window after pasting
Verify: swift build compiles

tasks.title: App Integration
tasks.description: Wire all managers together in AppDelegate and connect the status bar to popup and settings.
steps: Create Sources/Pasty/AppDelegate.swift as NSApplicationDelegate
Instantiate: ClipboardManager, StatusBarController, HotkeyManager, PopupWindowController
Update PastyApp.swift: use @NSApplicationDelegateAdaptor to connect AppDelegate
In applicationDidFinishLaunching: start clipboard monitoring, register hotkey, set up status bar
Wire StatusBarController.onShowHistory → toggle popup with clipboard history
Wire StatusBarController.onShowSettings → open settings window (NSWindow with NSHostingView of SettingsView)
Wire StatusBarController.onQuit → NSApplication.shared.terminate
Wire HotkeyManager.onHotkeyPressed → toggle popup
Wire PopupView.onSelect → ClipboardManager.pasteItem → hide popup
Verify: swift build compiles, swift run Pasty launches and shows menu bar icon

tasks.title: Polish and Test
tasks.description: End-to-end testing, edge case handling, and UI polish.
steps: Test full flow: copy text → appears in history → Cmd+Shift+V → select item → pastes correctly
Test edge cases: empty clipboard, very long text, rapid successive copies
Test settings: change max history size → verify history is trimmed, toggle hotkey → verify register/unregister
Polish popup: proper sizing, smooth animations, keyboard navigation (arrow keys + Enter)
Ensure menu bar icon appears correctly and all menu items work
Verify no compiler warnings
Run swift test — all tests pass
Create PROGRESS/polish-and-test.md documenting the final state
